input: December 30  Watch as Animal Dance Kingdom takes the stage at the JFK Library. The ensemble teaches audiences about animals and nature through fun facts and movement while encouraging attendees to join in. All ages are welcome to learn and move together, then stay to check out the library’s family-friendly Presidential Pets exhibit (adult admission fee applies). Free. 10:30 to 11:30 a.m. Reserve a space at jfklibrary.org. December 31 Bundle up and head to the Boston Common’s Frog Pond Skating Spectacular. The show, put on by The Skating Club of Boston, features competitive figure skaters, soloists, groups, and more, all guaranteed to wow crowds. Performances at 3 p.m. and 5:30 p.m. Free. bostonfrogpond.com  December 31  Don’t miss a minute of programming as part of First Night Boston. The annual New Year’s Eve celebration includes a parade, two fireworks shows, an organ concert, saxophone quartet, and more throughout the city. Try out archery on the Common, or hop on the Greenway Carousel and watch a festive dance. Free. Starting at 10 a.m. Find the schedule at firstnightboston.org.  January 10  Join an instructor-led painting session with Positive Paint Parties to benefit Boston’s Forgotten Felines rescue center. Create a custom portrait of your cherished pet, mingle with others, and check out handmade cat toys for sale. Proceeds help provide food, supplies, and medical care to cats in need. Pet portraits start at $45. At the Hub Pub, in Boston. 4 to 7 p.m. Register at bostonsforgottenfelines.org.  January 10-11 Come to the third annual Let’s Celebrate! concerts, a series showcasing multicultural traditions through music. Singers will perform three 20-minute operas honoring Boston’s diversity. Inspiration for the vignettes comes from Mexico’s Black Christ at Chalma; Uzbekistan’s Navruz, a national holiday celebrating the new year and new life; and Spain’s tomato-throwing festival. BCA Plaza Theatre. Saturday 7:30 p.m.; Sunday 2 p.m. Find tickets, which are pay what you wish, at whitesnakeprojects.org.  EDITOR’S NOTE: This edition of Your Week Ahead covers two weeks. Look for the next Globe Magazine on January 11.
